what_it_does: A large CSS stylesheet for the Studio design-system registry, providing visual styling for shared primitives, type cards, filters, trees, a type builder, a workshop studio floor, an architecture canvas, and family-specific thumbnail previews.
observations: Uses warm-ivory/gold CSS custom-property tokens throughout.
Defines many BEM-ish class families: cv-* (registry/components), tb-* (type builder), rs-* (workshop studio floor), cv-thumb-* (preview thumbnails), cv-arch-* (architecture canvas).
Card and tile layouts are thumb-first: .cv-type-card, .cv-reg-count-tile, .rs-tile, and .cv-template-card all embed a scaled .cv-thumb preview.
Encodes a layer taxonomy (token, atom, block, system, surface, doc, template) and provenance states (built-in, user, vi, imported) into class names, border accents, and pills.
The architecture canvas includes diagrammatic layouts: ladder rungs, slot examples, lineage trees, types-vs-templates comparison, consumers table, entry points, and cross-embed flows.
Universal TypeThumb section renders miniature UI mockups purely in CSS (fake dashboards, slides, wizards, brochures, color swatches, status dots).
Workshop floor section adds shelves, horizontal scrolling tile tracks, a slide-in inspector, a floating action button, and a Vi chat rail.
Uses :has() to conditionally restyle workshop tiles when a thumb is present.
Count tiles use a 7-column grid with a responsive fallback to 4 columns under 1200px.
Many thumb-embedding rules use !important to override base border/radius styles.
standouts: Thumb-first discovery pattern: nearly every registry/builder/workshop surface leads with a visual preview rather than text.
CSS-rendered, family-specific thumbnail mockups that stand in for real component renders or images.
Workshop Studio Floor (rs-shell) with its shelf/tile/inspector/FAB metaphor, distinct from the older registry grid.
Architecture canvas that doubles as explanatory diagrams (ladder, lineage, types-vs-templates) in addition to UI chrome.
conventions: CSS custom properties for color, typography, spacing, radius, and shadow tokens.
Repeated card anatomy: thumb-wrap, overlay, head/meta, desc, foot, use button.
Responsive media queries for count tiles and architecture rungs.
Use of aspect-ratio, object-fit-like scaling via absolute positioning, and overflow hidden for thumbnails.
concerns: Monolithic file mixing registry, builder, workshop, architecture, and thumb subsystems may be hard to maintain.
Frequent !important in .cv-thumb embedding rules could complicate future theming or overrides.
Hard-coded hex colors (#fff, #ddd, #555, #988058, #5B4628, #1F1A12) appear alongside token variables.
Count tile grid is hard-coded to seven layers; taxonomy changes require CSS edits.
:has() selectors may fail in older browsers.
Inspector fixed width of 480px and floor negative margin may cause viewport issues on small screens.
Many magic numbers for sizes, aspect ratios, and thumbnail scale factors are scattered throughout.
notes: A dense, thumb-first CSS manifest for the Studio registry/builder/workshop UI that encodes the layer taxonomy and provenance states into visual tokens.
questions: Which runtime framework/components consume these classes?
Where are the referenced CSS custom properties (e.g., --accent-gold, --font-display) defined?
Is the seven-layer taxonomy fixed in code or generated from a data schema?
Are the .cv-thumb previews rendered purely by CSS, by React components, or by generated images?
Is :has() support acceptable for the target browser matrix?
How is the 480px fixed inspector intended to behave on narrow viewports?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header says it styles the Universal Type Registry and new canvases, and the file indeed covers registry, type builder, workshop floor, architecture canvas, and universal thumbs.
apparent_themes: Progressive disclosure via collapsible stations, slide-in inspector, and modals
intention_signals: Build a unified visual language and layout system for a design-system registry where types are browsable via thumbnails, editable through a builder/inspector, and explained through architecture diagrams.
novelty: Embedding rich, family-specific UI mockups directly inside CSS as scaled thumbnail previews rather than relying on image assets or runtime component renders.
contribution: A comprehensive, token-driven stylesheet that defines the visual shell, card and thumb layouts, layer/provenance badges, and responsive canvases for the registry/builder/workshop experience; the thumb-first preview pattern and architecture diagram styles are reusable visual conventions.
summary_for_embedding: app/registry.css is a large CSS stylesheet for the Studio design-system registry, defining warm-ivory/gold UI components, thumb-first type cards, builder/inspector layouts, workshop floor shelves, architecture canvas diagrams, and family-specific CSS-rendered thumbnail previews.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give the Universal Type Registry and its builder/workshop canvases a consistent, visual, layer-aware interface for browsing, editing, and composing design-system types.
